checks.question: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?
checks.guidance: Cross-check the result against explicit user notes, critique corrections, and watch items. Flag anything the implementation ignored, contradicted, or only partially covered.

findings.detail: Checked the revised compatibility contract against the actual backdrop consumers. The plan now explicitly preserves `data-dialog-backdrop` and names `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useLightboxTransition.ts`, `src/styles/components/interactions.css`, and `src/index.css`, so the earlier backdrop-marker requirement from the prior critique is genuinely covered.

The revision still leaves one approved-plan requirement only partially operationalized: it says `LoraSelectorModal.tsx`, `useLightboxNavigation.ts`, and `useLightboxShellInteractionHandlers.ts` will move from DOM/z-index checks to `overlayStack` selectors keyed by overlay identity, but the current callers only own a `contentRef` or callback props and the plan never adds a context or helper that exposes a wrapper-generated overlay id back to those consumers. Without that bridge, the named caller migrations are not yet execution-ready even though the root-cause diagnosis remains correct.

prior_findings.detail: Checked the current overlay ownership files and helper callers against the revised steps. The plan now explicitly moves `src/domains/media-lightbox/components/LightboxShell.tsx` onto a sanctioned wrapper path under `src/shared/components/ui/overlay/`, and it now names the real helper-based `container` callers in `CommunityLorasTab.tsx`, `MyLorasTab.tsx`, and `sharedSections.tsx`, so the earlier LightboxShell and content-marker gaps are actually addressed.

Overlay markers are still only partially covered by the revised compatibility contract. The plan now preserves `data-dialog-content` and `data-lightbox-popup`, but the repository also has live code in `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useLightboxTransition.ts` and CSS in `src/styles/components/interactions.css` / `src/index.css` that key off `[data-dialog-backdrop]`; because the revision does not explicitly preserve that backdrop marker while centralizing backdrop ownership, it still leaves one approved-plan requirement only partially covered.

checks.question: Are the proposed changes technically correct?
checks.guidance: Look for logic errors, invalid assumptions, broken invariants, schema mismatches, or behavior that would fail at runtime. When the fix adds a conditional branch, check whether it handles all relevant cases — not just the one reported in the issue.

findings.detail: Checked `src/domains/lora/components/LoraSelectorModal/LoraSelectorModal.tsx`, `src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/useSharedLightboxState.ts`, and `src/domains/media-lightbox/components/LightboxShell.tsx`. Those call sites currently gate behavior through `getTopDialogElement()` or `hasDialogAbove()`, but none of them receives an overlay id from the wrapper layer: `useLightboxNavigation()` only gets callbacks, and `useLightboxShellInteractionHandlers()` only gets booleans plus `onClose`. As written, the stack-based replacement is technically incomplete until the plan defines how wrapper registration publishes the current overlay identity or topmost state to these consumers.

prior_findings.detail: The LightboxShell contract itself now lines up with the revised plan: `src/domains/media-lightbox/components/LightboxShell.tsx` really does depend on `modal={isActuallyModal}`, `disablePointerDismissal`, `eventDetails.cancel()` / `allowPropagation()`, and custom backdrop and popup handlers, so the new dedicated lightbox-wrapper step is technically the right shape for this repository.

The revised technical contract is still incomplete around backdrop identity. `useLightboxTransition.ts` clears `document.body.style.overflow` only when no `[data-dialog-backdrop]` remains, and the lightbox transition CSS also targets `[data-dialog-backdrop]`; since the plan only makes `data-dialog-content` / `data-lightbox-popup` marker stability explicit, a literal implementation could migrate dialog and lightbox backdrops into new wrappers but silently break this cleanup path.

checks.question: Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?
checks.guidance: Look at how the changed function is used across the codebase. Does the fix only address one caller's scenario while others remain broken? Flag missing required work or out-of-scope edits. A minimal patch is often right, but check whether the underlying problem is bigger than what the issue describes.

findings.detail: Search turned up another live overlay-detection path outside the marker helpers named in the plan: `src/shared/lib/interactions/elementPolicy.ts` classifies floating overlays via `[role="listbox"], [role="menu"], [data-side], [data-popup]`, and `src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/useLightboxShellInteractionHandlers.ts` uses that helper to decide whether content pointer events should be swallowed. Because M2 centralizes Select, Menu, Tooltip, Popover, and PreviewCard ownership, this helper is part of the same overlay-surface concept, but the revised scope does not mention preserving or migrating it.

prior_findings.detail: I checked the broader overlay surface with ripgrep and the revision correctly broadened scope to the real raw owners: there are 9 current raw Base UI import sites under `src/`, including the shared wrappers, `command.tsx`, and `LightboxShell.tsx`, so the plan is no longer narrowly focused on just the original Model Select path.

The broader support surface also includes non-wrapper consumers of overlay markers beyond the three `container` helpers. In particular, `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useLightboxTransition.ts` and `src/shared/hooks/useSlidingPane.ts` both consult live overlay DOM markers, but only the content-marker helper callers are called out in the migration checklist; the backdrop-marker consumer is therefore still outside the named scope even though wrapper centralization can affect it.

checks.question: Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?
checks.guidance: Search for all instances of the symbol/pattern being changed. Also ask: does this feature require setup, registration, or integration code beyond the core logic? Missing glue code causes test failures even when the core fix is correct.

findings.detail: Additional supporting infrastructure still hangs off popup-marker assumptions outside the files named in the plan. `src/shared/lib/interactions/elementPolicy.ts` and `src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMobileInteractions.ts` both key behavior off `[data-popup]` or related floating-overlay markers, so if the new overlay wrappers change DOM marker exposure or portal structure those paths need an explicit preserve-or-migrate decision too; the checklist currently stops at `useSlidingPane.ts` and the container helpers.

prior_findings.detail: The revision now covers the main infrastructure that held the old ordering model: `uiLayers.ts`, `uiLayers.test.ts`, `useLightboxShellInteractionHandlers.ts`, `useLightboxNavigation.ts`, `LoraSelectorModal.tsx`, and the three helper-based `SelectContent` container resolvers are all explicitly named, which matches the repository layout I inspected.

One supporting-infrastructure gap remains: the repo still has backdrop-marker integrations in `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useLightboxTransition.ts`, `src/styles/components/interactions.css`, and `src/index.css`, all of which reference `[data-dialog-backdrop]`. The revised plan's compatibility checklist mentions content markers and CSS tied to `[data-dialog-content]`, but it still does not name the backdrop marker or the transition hook that depends on it.

checks.question: Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?
checks.guidance: Grep for call sites. For each caller, check what values it passes — especially edge cases like None, zero, empty, or composite inputs. Then ask: should this change be here, or in a caller, callee, or new method?

findings.detail: Checked the actual topmost-gating caller contracts. `LoraSelectorModal.tsx` compares `getTopDialogElement()` to a local `contentRef`, `useSharedLightboxState.ts` calls `useLightboxNavigation({ onNext, onPrevious, onClose })`, and `LightboxShell.tsx` calls `useLightboxShellInteractionHandlers({ hasCanvasOverlay, isRepositionMode, isMobile, onClose })`. Since none of these callers passes an overlay id, wrapper handle, or registration token, the plan does not yet handle all caller shapes for the proposed `useIsOverlayTopmost(id)` / stack-selector replacement.

prior_findings.detail: I re-checked the helper-based popup callers named by the prior flag. `CommunityLorasTab.tsx`, `MyLorasTab.tsx`, and `sharedSections.tsx` are the actual `SelectContent container={...}` callers that derive their container from `[data-dialog-content]` or `[data-lightbox-popup]`, so the revised shared-helper and marker-preservation steps now match the real caller contracts.

There is still another concrete caller contract tied to overlay markers: `useLightboxTransition.ts` calls `document.querySelector('[data-dialog-backdrop]')` before deciding whether to clear `document.body.style.overflow`. Because the revised plan does not explicitly preserve or replace that backdrop marker contract, this caller can still break even if the `container` callers are handled correctly.

flags.concern: Overlay identity propagation: the plan replaces DOM topmost checks with `overlayStack` selectors, but current Lora and lightbox callers do not receive a wrapper-generated overlay id or context, so the migration path for `getTopDialogElement()` / `hasDialogAbove()` is still under-specified.
flags.evidence: `src/domains/lora/components/LoraSelectorModal/LoraSelectorModal.tsx` only has `contentRef`; `src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/useSharedLightboxState.ts` calls `useLightboxNavigation({ onNext, onPrevious, onClose })`; `src/domains/media-lightbox/components/LightboxShell.tsx` calls `useLightboxShellInteractionHandlers({ hasCanvasOverlay, isRepositionMode, isMobile, onClose })`.

flags.concern: Overlay interaction helpers: `elementPolicy.ts` and `useMobileInteractions.ts` still hardcode popup/menu DOM markers, but the revised plan does not explicitly preserve or migrate those supporting paths alongside the new overlay wrappers.
flags.evidence: `src/shared/lib/interactions/elementPolicy.ts` matches `[role="listbox"], [role="menu"], [data-side], [data-popup]`, and `src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMobileInteractions.ts` closes popovers based on `target.closest('[data-popup]')`.

flags.concern: Criterion 14: requires human verification (drive_browser, inspect_runtime_ui, verify_physical_device).
